compactKernelEllipticityStatus.label: compact commuted-kernel Sobolev ellipticity
compactKernelEllipticityStatus.reason: False as a continuum theorem.  Compactly supported oscillatory packets in ker R make the compact commuted-kernel form vanish relative to W^{m,2}; the notes and obstruction certificate rule out proving coercivity from this object alone.

finiteCommutedDominationStatus.label: finite commuted domination
finiteCommutedDominationStatus.reason: Finite Galerkin evidence shows subunit domination for suitable cutoff/order pairs, but this uses the compact commuted matrix and does not itself prove the continuum trace theorem.

sourceRangeHardyGreenStatus.label: continuum source-range Hardy/Green estimate
sourceRangeHardyGreenStatus.reason: Prove the continuum active trace-to-source range inclusion and the continuum source-inactive Hardy/Schur tail bound for the actual source rows. Do not require separate boundedness of all endpoint jet functionals.

rieszRepresenterEquivalenceStatus.label: A-Riesz representer equivalence
rieszRepresenterEquivalenceStatus.reason: In a Hilbert space, E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A is equivalent to the scalar source rows being A-bounded; the optimal eta_u is the top eigenvalue of the Gram matrix of their A-Riesz representers.

finiteGreenRepresenterStatus.label: finite Galerkin Green representers
finiteGreenRepresenterStatus.reason: Finite closed-trace high-block representers solve the A-Riesz range equations to roundoff in the current Galerkin model.

sourceWindowDerivativeCoverStatus.label: finite source-window derivative covering
sourceWindowDerivativeCoverStatus.reason: The analytic u-derivative source rows give a finite-net covering bound over the source window; this is evidence for uniformity, not the continuum representer construction itself.

continuumGreenRepresenterStatus.label: continuum closed-trace Green representers
continuumGreenRepresenterStatus.reason: Prove the continuum active trace-to-source range inclusion and the continuum source-inactive Hardy/Schur tail bound for the actual source rows. Do not require separate boundedness of all endpoint jet functionals.

directSourceRowDefinitionStatus.label: direct source-row definition
directSourceRowDefinitionStatus.reason: The source rows are the two explicit Lagrange/adjoint rows ell_{u,1}=B_P[h_u,.](s0) and ell_{u,2}=(P^*h_u)(s0)ev_{s0}.

finiteDirectRepresenterStatus.label: finite direct source-row Green representers
finiteDirectRepresenterStatus.reason: Finite Galerkin sections solve the A-Riesz range equations for the actual source rows to roundoff.

activeTraceRangeInclusionStatus.label: active interval trace range inclusion
activeTraceRangeInclusionStatus.reason: Finite active blocks satisfy E_active=C_sample R_global to roundoff, and the sampled density refinement is stable. The continuum trace-to-source Green kernel has not yet been proved.

sourceInactiveTailStatus.label: source-inactive Hardy/Schur tail
sourceInactiveTailStatus.reason: The finite full-Phi inactive source bound is below the Schur budget, but the continuum high-block Hardy/Schur tail passage is still open.

directGreenRepresenterStatus.label: continuum direct Green representers
directGreenRepresenterStatus.reason: By Riesz, g_{u,k} exists once ell_{u,k} is A-bounded on the completed high closed-trace space. The remaining proof is active trace range inclusion plus the source-inactive tail estimate.

uniformSourceGramBoundStatus.label: uniform 2x2 source Gram bound
uniformSourceGramBoundStatus.reason: Equivalent to a uniform source-row Hardy/Green bound sup_u ||E_u||_{A->C^2}<infinity. It follows after the direct representer theorem is closed.

fixedJetSufficientRouteStatus.label: fixed-jet sufficient route
fixedJetSufficientRouteStatus.reason: The decomposition through k_j^hi is algebraically sufficient, but finite fixed-jet norms grow sharply; the proof should keep the special source rows together.

continuumFixedJetRepresenterStatus.label: fixed-jet sufficient route
continuumFixedJetRepresenterStatus.reason: The decomposition through k_j^hi is algebraically sufficient, but finite fixed-jet norms grow sharply; the proof should keep the special source rows together.

finiteActiveRangeIdentityStatus.label: finite active range identity
finiteActiveRangeIdentityStatus.reason: Finite active blocks satisfy E_active=C_N R_N to roundoff.

sampledGreenDensityStatus.label: sampled Green density construction
sampledGreenDensityStatus.reason: Rows of C_N define sampled kernels K_N(a_i)=C_{N,i}/w_i.

traceGridDensityStabilityStatus.label: trace-grid density stability
traceGridDensityStabilityStatus.reason: Weighted density norms remain stable under trace refinement; this is finite evidence for an L2 trace-dual kernel.

lagrangeAdjointIdentityStatus.label: Lagrange adjoint identity
lagrangeAdjointIdentityStatus.reason: The symbolic identity D_a B_P[h,f]=h Lambda_a(f)-f P^*h is verified to roundoff for source test rows.

continuumAdjointGreenProblemStatus.label: continuum adjoint Green problem
continuumAdjointGreenProblemStatus.reason: The endpoint part is now reduced exactly to the fundamental-matrix Fredholm range condition.  The remaining global input is active trace range compatibility plus the source-inactive Hardy/Schur tail.

sampledKernelAdjointIbpStatus.label: sampled-kernel adjoint IBP diagnostic
sampledKernelAdjointIbpStatus.reason: Differentiating the coarse pseudoinverse kernel K_N does not solve the adjoint boundary problem; this diagnostic is expected to stay open until K_u is constructed by the BVP itself.

continuumTraceToSourceGreenKernel.remainingAnalyticGap: Prove the closed-trace active unique-continuation lemma in the continuum space and combine it with the source-inactive high-frequency Hardy/Schur tail.  The endpoint ODE step itself is now an exact fundamental-matrix/Fredholm reduction.

adjointBoundaryDiagnostic.interpretation: Finite-difference P^*K_N and endpoint concomitant diagnostic. Large IBP defects indicate the need for an analytic adjoint Green construction rather than differentiating coarse sampled kernel coefficients.

exactActiveRangeOnRegularizedKernel.label: exact active range on regularized kernels
exactActiveRangeOnRegularizedKernel.reason: Polynomial densities solve the two active moment equations.

regularizedAdjointIbpSmall.label: regularized adjoint IBP diagnostic
regularizedAdjointIbpSmall.reason: Tests whether the smooth finite kernels behave like solutions of P^*K=eta with matched endpoint concomitant.

continuumBvpClosed.label: continuum adjoint BVP compactness
continuumBvpClosed.reason: Still requires a uniform norm bound and convergence of the regularized kernels in the trace-dual topology.

activeEndpointMapFullRowRank.label: active endpoint map full row rank
activeEndpointMapFullRowRank.reason: Sufficient but not necessary; the diagnostic may still close if the actual endpoint right-hand sides lie in Range(M).

actualEndpointRhsInRange.label: actual endpoint RHS in Range(M)
actualEndpointRhsInRange.reason: For the actual source rows, the endpoint vectors b(d) are in the finite endpoint-map range.

activeEndpointKilled.label: active endpoint concomitant killed
activeEndpointKilled.reason: Minimum-norm z solves Mz=-b(d) on the active two-dimensional source space.

uniformTraceDualBoundClosed.label: uniform trace-dual bound
uniformTraceDualBoundClosed.reason: Still needs a rigorous continuum bound for the exact fundamental matrix and M^+ over the source window.

exactFundamentalMatrixEndpointMapStatus.label: exact fundamental-matrix endpoint map
exactFundamentalMatrixEndpointMapStatus.reason: For analytic coefficients with nonzero leading coefficient, P^*K=0 has a unique fundamental matrix Phi(a,t), and the endpoint concomitant is exactly Pi_delta Beta_z=Mz+b(d).

endpointFredholmRangeAlternativeStatus.label: endpoint Fredholm range alternative
endpointFredholmRangeAlternativeStatus.reason: The exact endpoint equation Mz=-b(d) is solvable iff b(d) is orthogonal to ker(M^T); the minimum-norm solution is z=-M^+b(d).

uniformTraceDualBoundFromCompactnessStat.label: uniform trace-dual bound from compactness
uniformTraceDualBoundFromCompactnessStat.reason: If rank M is constant, b(d_u) lies in Range(M), and d_u is continuous on the compact source window, then ||M^+b(d_u)|| and the trace-dual norm of the piecewise homogeneous Green solution are uniformly bounded.

finiteEndpointCompatibilityEvidenceStatu.label: finite endpoint compatibility evidence
finiteEndpointCompatibilityEvidenceStatu.reason: The sampled-flow diagnostic has rank one on the two active modes, but the actual endpoint right-hand sides lie in its range and are killed by the Moore-Penrose z choice.

continuumActiveRangeCompatibilityStatus.label: continuum active range compatibility
continuumActiveRangeCompatibilityStatus.reason: Open until the compactness/Mosco passage and the closed-trace active unique-continuation lemma are proved.

hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.label: Hilbert annihilator range criterion
hilbertAnnihilatorRangeCriterionStatus.reason: For a densely defined closed trace map R between Hilbert spaces, closure Ran(R^*)=(ker R)^perp.  Hence E_active factors through the interval trace iff ker R is annihilated by E_active.

finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.label: finite active block factorization
finiteActiveBlockFactorizationStatus.reason: Current Galerkin active blocks satisfy E_active=C_sample R_global to roundoff.  This is finite evidence for, not proof of, the continuum annihilator condition.

compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.label: compactness contradiction reduction
compactnessContradictionReductionStatus.reason: Failure of ker R subset ker E_active is equivalent to an A-normalized sequence with R f_n -> 0 and active source bounded below.  The high-block elliptic/Mosco theorem is the compactness mechanism needed to pass such a sequence to a continuum limit.

closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.label: closed-trace active unique continuation
closedTraceActiveUniqueContinuationStatu.reason: Closed if the exact Green annihilation implication is available for every scalar active source row.  The remaining open part is endpoint/Fredholm compatibility in the continuum exact Green BVP.

continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.label: continuum active trace range compatibility
continuumActiveTraceRangeCompatibilitySt.reason: Open until the compactness/Mosco passage and the closed-trace active unique-continuation lemma are proved.

activeTraceRangeTheorem.remainingAnalyticGap: Prove endpoint/Fredholm compatibility for the exact continuum Green BVP without appealing to sampled active range inclusion.  Equivalently, show every homogeneous endpoint obstruction annihilates the actual active source rows, using the Volterra/Sturm structure.

formalConstruction: Let H_hi be the A-Hilbert completion of the high block after global trace closure and finite low/mid removal.
If ell_{u,k} is A-bounded on H_hi, set g_{u,k}=J_A^{-1}ell_{u,k}, where J_A:H_hi->H_hi^* is the Riesz map induced by <.,.>_A.
The active part is to be represented by an interval kernel K_u with P_delta E_u=integral K_u(a)Lambda_a da; it vanishes on ker R_global.
The inactive part is bounded by the high-frequency Hardy/Schur tail. Hence the restricted source rows have Riesz representers in H_hi.
For G_ij(u)=<g_{u,i},g_{u,j}>_A, lambda_max(G(u))=||E_u||_{A->C^2}^2; a uniform Hardy/Green bound gives sup_{0.08<=u<=0.52} lambda_max(G(u))<infinity.
continuumGreenRepresenterTheorem.remainingAnalyticGap: Prove the continuum active trace-to-source range inclusion and the continuum source-inactive Hardy/Schur tail bound for the actual source rows. Do not require separate boundedness of all endpoint jet functionals.
continuumGreenRepresenterTheorem.interpretation: The continuum Green representers should be constructed directly as A-Riesz representers of the two Lagrange source rows. Finite direct representers and active trace-to-source factorizations are exact to roundoff; the continuum range/tail theorem remains open.

formalProof: Let H_hi be H_M cap ker R_global with inner product <f,g>_A=<Af,g>.  For fixed u, write E_u f=(ell_1(f),ell_2(f)).
If ell_k(f)=<g_k,f>_A for g_k in H_hi, then ||E_u f||^2 <= lambda_max((<g_i,g_j>_A)) ||f||_A^2 by Cauchy-Schwarz in the finite-dimensional representer span.
Conversely, E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A makes each ell_k A-bounded, so the Riesz theorem supplies the representers.  Thus the estimate and Green representer theorem are equivalent.
Uniform source-window control follows if u -> g_{u,k} is continuous in H_A and the representer Gram is bounded on the compact source window.
sourceRangeHardyGreenTheorem.remainingAnalyticGap: Prove the continuum active trace-to-source range inclusion and the continuum source-inactive Hardy/Schur tail bound for the actual source rows. Do not require separate boundedness of all endpoint jet functionals.
sourceRangeHardyGreenTheorem.interpretation: The source-range Hardy/Green theorem is now reduced to a precise continuum Green representer construction.  Finite representers and source-window derivative bounds are strong evidence, but the continuum estimate is still open until those representers are constructed and uniformly bounded.

compactSourceExhaustionStatus.label: compact-source high-block exhaustion implication
compactSourceExhaustionStatus.reason: The corrected high-block proof is a compact-source/Mosco theorem, not a raw compact-kernel Sobolev coercivity theorem.  It closes the functional-analytic implication conditional on the continuum trace-frame lower bound.

activeSourceInputStatus.label: active source annihilation/range input
activeSourceInputStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_theorem.py: R_global f=0 implies P_active Ehat f=0, equivalently E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*).

certifiedSourceModelStatus.label: certified normalized source model input
certifiedSourceModelStatus.reason: The finite/certified full-theta source model gives the normalized inactive-tail constant and verifies it is below the low/mid Schur budget.

abstractMoscoLimsupStatus.label: abstract Mosco limsup with bounded trace correction
abstractMoscoLimsupStatus.reason: If V_N is A-graph dense, sampled traces converge on V_N, and the sampled trace map has a uniformly bounded right inverse, then each f in H_M has corrected approximants f_N in H_{M,N}.  The correction norm is bounded by gamma_0^{-1/2}||R_N v_N|| and tends to zero.

abstractMoscoLiminfStatus.label: abstract Mosco liminf from weak compactness and trace consistency
abstractMoscoLiminfStatus.reason: If f_N is A-bounded in H_{M,N}, Hilbert weak compactness gives a subsequence.  Trace consistency passes R_N f_N=0 to R_global f=0, and finite low/mid A-orthogonality passes by strong convergence of the removed modes.

compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.label: compact source operator norm convergence
compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.reason: Mosco convergence of closed subspaces is equivalent to strong convergence of the A-orthogonal projections.  For compact S=Ehat^*Ehat, strong projection convergence gives ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S||_{A->A}->0.

minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.label: continuum min-max passage
minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.reason: Norm convergence of compact positive source operators implies convergence of lambda_3 and of the spectral min-max tail.  The continuum P_2 estimate follows from Courant-Fischer.

finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.label: finite trace-frame evidence
finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.reason: The finite weighted trace frames have positive observed lower bound and stable trace-mesh refinement.  This is numerical evidence for, not a standalone analytic proof of, the continuum uniform frame lower bound.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from continuum_trace_frame_lower_bound_theorem.py: active range plus source noncollapse make R_global injective on the finite active spectral space; compactness gives gamma_delta>0, and finite-dimensional quadrature consistency gives bounded sampled-trace correction right inverses for fine trace meshes.

sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.label: full-Phi active source noncollapse
sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.reason: Imported from full_theta_source_quadrature_certificate.py.  It proves that E_active is injective on the active source spectral space after continuum source quadrature and theta-tail errors.

activeRangeStatus.label: closed active range implication
activeRangeStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_theorem.py: E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*), equivalently R_global f=0 implies E_active f=0.

traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.label: R_global injective on H_delta
traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.reason: If R_global f=0 on H_delta, active range gives E_active f=0.  Source-side noncollapse makes E_active injective on H_delta, so f=0.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum L2 trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: The A-unit sphere in finite-dimensional H_delta is compact, and f -> ||Lambda_.(f)||_{L2(I)} is continuous and nonzero there by trace injectivity.  Its minimum gamma_delta is therefore positive.

traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.label: uniform trace quadrature consistency on H_delta
traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.reason: On finite-dimensional H_delta, the family |Lambda_a(f)|^2 with ||f||_A=1 is compact/equicontinuous in C(I).  Any consistent positive trace quadrature converges uniformly on it, so discrete frame matrices converge to the continuum frame matrix.

boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.label: bounded sampled-trace correction right inverse on active block
boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.reason: For sufficiently fine trace meshes, the discrete frame lower bound is at least gamma_delta/2.  The Moore-Penrose right inverse of W^{1/2}R_N on the active trace block then has norm at most sqrt(2/gamma_delta).

explicitNumericGammaStatus.label: explicit rigorous numeric gamma_delta
explicitNumericGammaStatus.reason: The theorem proves gamma_delta>0 qualitatively.  The observed finite floor is recorded, but an interval quadrature error bound would be needed to certify a numerical lower value such as the observed frame floor.

proof: Let H_delta be the finite-dimensional active source spectral space for the compact source operator S=Ehat^*Ehat.
Suppose f in H_delta and Lambda_a(f)=0 for every a in I.  Then R_global f=0, so the synchronized active range theorem gives E_active f=0.
The full-Phi source-side noncollapse theorem says E_active is injective on H_delta.  Hence f=0.  Therefore the continuum trace map is injective on H_delta.
The function q(f)=int_I |Lambda_a(f)|^2 da is continuous on the compact A-unit sphere of H_delta and is strictly positive there.  Thus gamma_delta=min q(f)>0.

Because H_delta is finite dimensional and a -> Lambda_a(f) is continuous, positive quadrature rules converge uniformly on the compact family q_f(a)=|Lambda_a(f)|^2.  The discrete weighted frame matrices converge to the continuum frame matrix.
For all sufficiently fine trace meshes, the discrete smallest frame eigenvalue is at least gamma_delta/2.  The sampled trace correction right inverse is therefore bounded by sqrt(2/gamma_delta).
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundTheorem.formalConclusion: There exists gamma_delta>0 such that int_I |Lambda_a(f)|^2 da >= gamma_delta ||f||_A^2 for every f in H_delta.  The corresponding sampled weighted trace frames have a positive lower bound for all sufficiently fine meshes, giving a bounded sampled-trace correction right inverse on the active finite-dimensional trace block.
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundTheorem.remainingNumericalGap: Only an explicit numerical gamma_delta remains open.  The qualitative continuum lower bound and quadrature consistency are closed by compactness/injectivity.

formalProof: Let H_A be the A-Hilbert completion, R=R_global, H_M=ker(R) cap L_M^{perp_A}, and H_{M,N}=V_N cap ker(R_N) cap L_{M,N}^{perp_A}.

Limsup: choose v_N in V_N with v_N->f in A graph norm.  Since Rf=0 and sampled traces are consistent, R_N v_N->0.  A uniform trace-frame lower bound gamma_0 supplies a correction w_N with R_N w_N=R_N v_N and ||w_N||_A<=gamma_0^{-1/2}||R_N v_N||.  Then f_N=v_N-w_N lies in ker R_N and f_N->f; finite low/mid orthogonality is corrected by the convergent finite Gram matrix.

Liminf: an A-bounded sequence f_N in H_{M,N} has a weakly convergent subsequence.  Trace consistency passes R_N f_N=0 to Rf=0, and low/mid orthogonality passes to the limit, so the weak limit belongs to H_M.

Mosco convergence gives strong convergence of the A-orthogonal projections Pi_N to Pi.  Since the source operator is compact after active range annihilation plus the inactive Hardy/Schur bound, ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S|| tends to zero.

Therefore lambda_3(S_N)->lambda_3(S).  The finite normalized inactive-tail bound passes to H_M, giving the same epsilon constant in the full closed trace space once the continuum trace-frame lower bound is supplied.
compactSourceExhaustionProof.conditionalConclusion: Assuming the continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound/quadrature consistency, the certified normalized estimate ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= 1.345452638275e-03<A f,f> passes to H_M cap ker R_global and is absorbed by the low/mid budget 5.730309711104e-03 with slack 4.384857072828e-03.

endpointTraceBoundednessStatus.label: endpoint source-row trace boundedness
endpointTraceBoundednessStatus.reason: Follows once the compact-source exhaustion theorem is unconditional; the current remaining input is the continuum trace-frame lower bound for sampled trace correction.

compactObstruction.interpretation: For the compact kernel model S_m^comm, smooth oscillatory packets supported away from the endpoint trace interval drive S_m/W_m toward zero. The continuum elliptic estimate therefore needs the local positive terms from the true commuted Sturm identity.

sourceRangeCertificate.interpretation: Full Sobolev W10 controls the residual but is not absorbable by the compact positive block. The finite source-range regularizer is the viable auxiliary object; its absorption constant is the direct high-block Schur/Douglas constant.

ellipticTraceTheorem.correctedTheorem: For the closed continuum trace space and after removing the finite low/mid A-modes, prove E_u^*E_u <= eta_u A uniformly in u on the source window.  This source-range Hardy/Green estimate is the elliptic trace input needed by the high-block exhaustion theorem.
ellipticTraceTheorem.invalidRoute: Do not try to prove raw local a_10(s)>0 Sobolev ellipticity for the compact endpoint commuted-kernel form; the compact packet obstruction disproves that route.
formalConsequencesIfClosed: Endpoint source rows are A-bounded on H_M cap ker R_global.
The normalized source operator Ehat_Phi is compact/finite-rank in the A-Hilbert source model.
P_N Ehat^*Ehat P_N -> Ehat^*Ehat follows once A-graph Galerkin/Mosco convergence is also proved.
The high-block exhaustion ledger can then close sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus after Mosco is supplied.
ellipticTraceTheorem.interpretation: The corrected elliptic trace target has been sharpened: the compact-kernel Sobolev route is invalid, while the compact-source exhaustion implication is proved conditionally.  The remaining non-formal input is the continuum trace-frame lower bound.

activeSourceInputStatus.label: active source annihilation/range input
activeSourceInputStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_theorem.py: R_global f=0 implies P_active Ehat f=0, equivalently E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*).

certifiedSourceModelStatus.label: certified normalized source model input
certifiedSourceModelStatus.reason: The finite/certified full-theta source model gives the normalized inactive-tail constant and verifies it is below the low/mid Schur budget.

abstractMoscoLimsupStatus.reason: If V_N is A-graph dense, sampled traces converge on V_N, and the sampled trace map has a uniformly bounded right inverse, then each f in H_M has corrected approximants f_N in H_{M,N}.  The correction norm is bounded by gamma_0^{-1/2}||R_N v_N|| and tends to zero.

abstractMoscoLiminfStatus.reason: If f_N is A-bounded in H_{M,N}, Hilbert weak compactness gives a subsequence.  Trace consistency passes R_N f_N=0 to R_global f=0, and finite low/mid A-orthogonality passes by strong convergence of the removed modes.

compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.label: compact source operator norm convergence
compactSourceNormConvergenceStatus.reason: Mosco convergence of closed subspaces is equivalent to strong convergence of the A-orthogonal projections.  For compact S=Ehat^*Ehat, strong projection convergence gives ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S||_{A->A}->0.

minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.label: continuum min-max passage
minmaxContinuumPassageStatus.reason: Norm convergence of compact positive source operators implies convergence of lambda_3 and of the spectral min-max tail.  The continuum P_2 estimate follows from Courant-Fischer.

finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.label: finite trace-frame evidence
finiteTraceFrameEvidenceStatus.reason: The finite weighted trace frames have positive observed lower bound and stable trace-mesh refinement.  This is numerical evidence for, not a standalone analytic proof of, the continuum uniform frame lower bound.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from continuum_trace_frame_lower_bound_theorem.py: active range plus source noncollapse make R_global injective on the finite active spectral space; compactness gives gamma_delta>0, and finite-dimensional quadrature consistency gives bounded sampled-trace correction right inverses for fine trace meshes.

sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.label: full-Phi active source noncollapse
sourceSideNoncollapseStatus.reason: Imported from full_theta_source_quadrature_certificate.py.  It proves that E_active is injective on the active source spectral space after continuum source quadrature and theta-tail errors.

activeRangeStatus.label: closed active range implication
activeRangeStatus.reason: Imported from synchronized_active_range_theorem.py: E_active lies in closure Range(R_global^*), equivalently R_global f=0 implies E_active f=0.

traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.label: R_global injective on H_delta
traceInjectivityOnActiveSpaceStatus.reason: If R_global f=0 on H_delta, active range gives E_active f=0.  Source-side noncollapse makes E_active injective on H_delta, so f=0.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum L2 trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: The A-unit sphere in finite-dimensional H_delta is compact, and f -> ||Lambda_.(f)||_{L2(I)} is continuous and nonzero there by trace injectivity.  Its minimum gamma_delta is therefore positive.

traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.label: uniform trace quadrature consistency on H_delta
traceQuadratureConsistencyStatus.reason: On finite-dimensional H_delta, the family |Lambda_a(f)|^2 with ||f||_A=1 is compact/equicontinuous in C(I).  Any consistent positive trace quadrature converges uniformly on it, so discrete frame matrices converge to the continuum frame matrix.

boundedSampledTraceCorrectionStatus.reason: For sufficiently fine trace meshes, the discrete frame lower bound is at least gamma_delta/2.  The Moore-Penrose right inverse of W^{1/2}R_N on the active trace block then has norm at most sqrt(2/gamma_delta).

explicitNumericGammaStatus.reason: The theorem proves gamma_delta>0 qualitatively.  The observed finite floor is recorded, but an interval quadrature error bound would be needed to certify a numerical lower value such as the observed frame floor.

compactSourceExhaustionProof.conditionalConclusion: Assuming the continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound/quadrature consistency, the certified normalized estimate ||(I-P_active)Ehat f||^2 <= 1.345452638275e-03<A f,f> passes to H_M cap ker R_global and is absorbed by the low/mid budget 5.730309711104e-03 with slack 4.384857072828e-03.

abstractCompactExhaustionStatus.label: abstract compact-source exhaustion theorem
abstractCompactExhaustionStatus.reason: The Hilbert-space Mosco/compact-source/min-max implication is closed.  It is conditional only on the continuum trace-frame lower bound needed to identify the sampled closed-trace Galerkin spaces with H_M.

continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.label: continuum uniform trace-frame lower bound
continuumTraceFrameLowerBoundStatus.reason: Imported from continuum_trace_frame_lower_bound_theorem.py: active range plus source noncollapse make R_global injective on the finite active spectral space; compactness gives gamma_delta>0, and finite-dimensional quadrature consistency gives bounded sampled-trace correction right inverses for fine trace meshes.

moscoLimsupStatus.label: Mosco limsup/recovery sequence
moscoLimsupStatus.reason: The compact-source proof gives the recovery sequence once the sampled trace map has a continuum uniform lower frame bound and bounded correction right inverse.

moscoLiminfStatus.label: Mosco liminf/compactness
moscoLiminfStatus.reason: The compact-source proof gives the weak compactness/trace-closure argument once sampled trace quadrature is known to converge on A-bounded high-block sequences.

ellipticTraceStatus.label: commuted Sturm/Volterra elliptic estimate
ellipticTraceStatus.reason: The compact-source exhaustion proof supplies the valid replacement for the invalid raw compact-kernel Sobolev route.

sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.label: source operator norm convergence
sourceOperatorNormConvergenceStatus.reason: The compact-source proof shows that Mosco convergence of the closed high blocks plus compactness of S=Ehat^*Ehat gives ||Pi_N S Pi_N-S||_{A->A}->0.

spectralProjectionConvergenceStatus.label: Riesz spectral projection convergence
spectralProjectionConvergenceStatus.reason: Standard consequence of ||P_N S P_N-S||_{A->A}->0 and an open gap around the top-two source spectrum.

minmaxPassageStatus.label: min-max inequality passage
minmaxPassageStatus.reason: Once source operator norm convergence and spectral projection convergence hold, the Courant-Fischer/Riesz projection argument passes the normalized tail inequality to the continuum.

formalProof: Define H_A as the Hilbert completion under the positive Volterra/Sturm form A, N=ker R_global, and H_M=N cap L_M^{perp_A}.
Let V_N be the polynomial Galerkin space and H_{M,N}=V_N cap ker R_N cap L_{M,N}^{perp_A}.  Mosco convergence H_{M,N}->H_M is the required exhaustion theorem.

The source row is the direct Lagrange/adjoint pair E_u f=(B_P[h_u,f](s0), (P^*h_u)(s0)f(s0)).  The source-range Hardy/Green theorem constructs its A-Riesz representers uniformly, so S=E^*E is compact in the A-Hilbert source model.
A-graph Galerkin convergence plus compact direct source-row factorization gives ||P_NSP_N-S||_{A->A}->0.
Riesz projection convergence then gives P_{2,N}->P_2, and the normalized finite min-max tail inequality passes to H_M cap ker R_global by recovery sequences and lower limits.
interpretation: The functional-analytic compact-source passage is proved, and the continuum trace-frame lower-bound theorem supplies the missing bounded correction right inverse.  The normalized tail estimate now passes to the full closed high block.
